nodes.analysis: The JSON output includes speaker diarization with three distinct speakers and timestamps, meeting the checkpoint requirement. However, the transcription excerpt is truncated, preventing full verification of speaker-turn annotation throughout the entire recording. The provided structure and labeling are correct.

The JSON output contains three research summaries, each under 150 words, with proper source URLs (CNN, NYT, Reuters) and access dates (2025-10-26). All articles are dated after 2024 and directly address the 2025 Stalin statue in the Moscow Metro, fulfilling the checkpoint requirements completely.

The checkpoint requires summarizing additional contextual articles (e.g., political reactions) with source URL and access date. The provided JSON contains three research summaries, but they focus on the statue unveiling itself, not additional contextual political reactions. No supplementary articles addressing political reactions are included, failing the checkpoint requirement.

The JSON contains placeholder text ('...transcription continues...') instead of the full transcription, and speaker diarization shows only three speakers with generic labels, not distinct identified individuals. The research summaries and emotion analysis appear plausible, but the incomplete transcription and lack of verified audio processing outputs severely undermine compliance.
